LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE DEC-17-2021 HEARING RE: DEFENDANT QUICKLY CORPORATION'S MOTION TO CONTEST DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES, INC.'S APPLICATION FOR GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. MOTION DENIED. (SEE WORD MINUTES FOR DETAILS.) COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion To Contest Defendant Abm Industries, Inc.?S Application For Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT QUICKLY CORPORATION's Motion To Contest Defendant ABM Industries, Inc.'s Application For Good Faith Settlement.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Quickly Corporation's motion to contest the settlement between plaintiff and ABM is denied. (See Word minutes for complete ruling.)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-17-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRY GROUPS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances are made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

ABM's motion for summary judgment is off calendar as moot. Quickly can no longer maintain its cross-complaint for equitable indemnity. (See CCP 877.6(c).

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-08-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-03-2021 Continued To Dec-17-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRY GROUPS, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter continued to December 17, 2021. ABM seeks summary judgment against plaintiff's complaint and Quickly Corp.'s cross-complaint for equitable indemnity/contribution. Quickly does not allege any express indemnity claims. Plaintiff and ABM entered into a settlement and ABM filed an application for determination of good faith settlement. Quickly's time to contest the good faith of the settlement pursuant to CCP 877.6(a)(2) will not run by the time of this summary judgment hearing. If the settlement is found to be in good faith, then Quickly's cross-claims for equitable indemnity/contribution will be barred. (See CCP 877.6(c).) Therefore, for the sake of judicial economy, the court orders this motion to be continued to December 17, 2021 and finds good cause for any motion to contest to also be set on that date at 9:30 a.m. (See CCP 877.6(a)(1).) If Quickly intends to contest the settlement, then it shall file and serve its motion to contest by December 10, 2021. Any opposition to that motion shall be filed and served by December 15, 2021.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
